qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OpenAPI path-parameter URL rewriting lets MCP callers escape the intended downstream target
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable function, `RequestDirector._build_url()` in `src/fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py`, and the same root cause as the advisory: raw path-parameter substitution before `urljoin()` with no URL encoding, which lets attacker-controlled path values escape the intended downstream target.

selected_finding.title: OpenAPI path-parameter URL rewriting lets MCP callers escape the intended downstream target

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding matches GHSA-vv7q-7jx5-f767 at the same vulnerable code and root cause, and the copied PoC produced end-to-end runtime evidence that attacker-controlled path input redirected an OpenAPI-backed request to an attacker-selected host while forwarding configured authentication headers.
evidence: The advisory describes `OpenAPIProvider` path-parameter substitution in `_build_url()` followed by `urljoin()` as the root cause in `fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py`.

The vulnerable checkout at commit `bafd5419fabbd30415c3b782e778ba971f8019e7` still has raw `url_path.replace(placeholder, str(param_value))` followed by `urljoin(...)` in `src/fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py`.

The fixing commit `40bdfb6b1de0ce30609ee9ba5bb95ecd04a9fb71` changes that same function to URL-encode path parameters before `urljoin()`, confirming the advisory and the finding are about the same root cause.

`/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_be32b2c2/.judge/poc_runs/openapi_path_parameter_url_rewrite/run.log` contains `EVOHUNT_PROOF: OpenAPITool sent the rewritten request to the attacker listener and leaked configured Authorization and X-Api-Key headers.`
The same run log also contains `EVOHUNT_NOTE: same-origin path traversal also rewrites /users/{id} with ../../admin/export to /admin/export.`

poc_executions.observed_effect: The adapted local verifier printed `EVOHUNT_PROOF` after `OpenAPITool` sent the rewritten request to the attacker listener and leaked the configured `Authorization` and `X-Api-Key` headers; it also printed `EVOHUNT_NOTE` confirming `../../admin/export` rewrites to `/admin/export`.
